question: Spread: Tromsø IL (-2.5)
description:
In the upcoming Norway Eliteserien game, scheduled for April 15 at 1:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Tromsø IL" if Tromsø IL win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Lillestrøm SK".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on eliteserien.no. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Tromsø IL vs. Lillestrøm SK - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Norway Eliteserien game, scheduled for April 15 at 1:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tromsø IL's dominant home record at frigid Alfheim Stadion anchors trader consensus favoring the hosts against struggling Lillestrøm SK in this Eliteserien matchup, with markets implying roughly 52-55% win probability for Tromsø. The northern side boasts a 4-1-0 mark in their last five home games, leveraging tough Arctic conditions that have tripped up visitors. Lillestrøm languishes in 13th place, winless in six straight away outings amid leaky defending and just one goal in their past four road matches. Official injury reports show both squads largely intact, though Lillestrøm's top scorer Thomas Lehne Olsen is questionable. Head-to-head trends support Tromsø, who've claimed victory in the last two home meetings.

question: Miami Open: Mariano Navone vs Valentin Vacherot
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Mariano Navone and Valentin Vacherot in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 20 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Mariano Navone' if Mariano Navone advances against Valentin Vacherot.

This market will resolve to 'Valentin Vacherot' if Valentin Vacherot advances against Mariano Navone.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Valentin Vacherot's scorching recent form anchors his 74% implied probability against Mariano Navone in the Miami Open first round, highlighted by a Challenger title in Szeged last week and stunning qualifier upsets, including a straight-sets win over top-20 seed Karen Khachanov on these outdoor hard courts. Navone, meanwhile, enters with tepid momentum, posting just a 4-6 record over his last 10 matches and a first-round exit at Indian Wells, where his clay-court strengths have translated unevenly to hard courts. Absent head-to-head history or reported injuries, trader consensus weighs Vacherot's qualifier edge and surface acclimation as pivotal momentum drivers amid the tournament's high-upset potential.

question: Ethereum Up or Down on March 21?
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT Mar 20 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is lower than the final "Close" price for the Mar 21 '26 12:00 ET candle.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT Mar 20 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is higher than the final "Close" price for the Mar 21 '26 12:00 ET candle.

If the final "Close" price for both of these candles is exactly equal on Binance, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: Solana Up or Down - March 21, 12PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the SOL/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: Solana Up or Down on March 21?
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT Mar 20 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is lower than the final "Close" price for the Mar 21 '26 12:00 ET candle.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT Mar 20 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is higher than the final "Close" price for the Mar 21 '26 12:00 ET candle.

If the final "Close" price for both of these candles is exactly equal on Binance, this market will resolve 50-50.

question: T20 International Diamond Premier League: Hong Kong (Idpl) vs India (Idpl)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Hong Kong (Idpl) and India (Idpl) scheduled for March 20 2026 in T20 International Diamond Premier League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the T20 International Diamond Premier League market strongly favors India IDPL (around 80% implied probability) over Hong Kong IDPL, driven by India's unbeaten run through three IDPL matches, including a 25-run win over Pakistan Spartans where their top order fired 200+. Hong Kong Heroes sit bottom of the table after heavy defeats, leaking runs via weak bowling and posting sub-140 totals. No major injuries reported from official lineups, but India's pace attack exploits Hong Kong's brittle middle order on Greater Noida's batting-friendly pitch. Head-to-head neutral as first meeting; watch for dew aiding second innings chase tonight.

question: T20 International Diamond Premier League: Hong Kong (Idpl) vs India (Idpl) - Who wins the toss?
description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Hong Kong (Idpl) and India (Idpl) scheduled for 2026-03-20 in T20 International Diamond Premier League.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

question: T20 International Diamond Premier League: Hong Kong (Idpl) vs India (Idpl) - Completed match?
description:
This market refers to whether the cricket match between Hong Kong (Idpl) and India (Idpl) scheduled for 2026-03-20 in T20 International Diamond Premier League is completed under the competition's official playing conditions.
This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
Any match where an official result is declared — including results decided by DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, a Super Over, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling — will resolve Yes. (Draws count as completed results when recognized by the competition.)
If the match is abandoned, ends in No Result, is permanently canceled, or otherwise concludes without an official result, the market will resolve No.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed or permanently canceled, after which it will resolve as described above.

question: T20 International Diamond Premier League: Hong Kong (Idpl) vs India (Idpl) - Toss Match Double Hong Kong (Idpl) Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Hong Kong (Idpl) and India (Idpl) scheduled for 2026-03-20 in T20 International Diamond Premier League.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Hong Kong (Idpl) will be considered correct if  Hong Kong (Idpl) is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to India (Idpl) will be considered correct if India (Idpl) is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment reflects razor-thin margins in this T20 International Diamond Premier League toss match double, with Hong Kong, India, and draw priced at even 50% implied probabilities amid high uncertainty over the coin flip's pivotal role. In dew-prone conditions typical of such night games, captains overwhelmingly opt to field first, amplifying toss winner's edge—historically boosting win odds by 10-15% in T20s. Both IDPL squads enter with balanced recent form: Hong Kong's explosive openers counter India's spin-heavy attack, while no major injuries reported per team updates keep lineups intact. Head-to-heads show no clear dominance, fueling the stalemate as weather risks a washout draw, underscoring why crowds price this as a pure coin-flip battle.

question: T20 International Diamond Premier League: Hong Kong (Idpl) vs India (Idpl) - Most Sixes Hong Kong (Idpl) Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Hong Kong (Idpl) and India (Idpl) scheduled for 2026-03-20 in T20 International Diamond Premier League.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Hong Kong (Idpl) will be considered correct if Hong Kong (Idpl) is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than India (Idpl).The outcome corresponding to India (Idpl) will be considered correct if India (Idpl) is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Hong Kong (Idpl).
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: T20 International Diamond Premier League: Hong Kong (Idpl) vs Belgium (Idpl)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Hong Kong (Idpl) and Belgium (Idpl) scheduled for March 21 2026 in T20 International Diamond Premier League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: T20 International Diamond Premier League: Hong Kong (Idpl) vs Belgium (Idpl) - Toss Match Double Hong Kong (Idpl) Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Hong Kong (Idpl) and Belgium (Idpl) scheduled for 2026-03-20 in T20 International Diamond Premier League.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Hong Kong (Idpl) will be considered correct if  Hong Kong (Idpl) is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Belgium (Idpl) will be considered correct if Belgium (Idpl) is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: T20 Nigeria Invitational Tournament, Women: Rwanda vs Zimbabwe
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Rwanda and Zimbabwe scheduled for March 21 2026 in T20 Nigeria Invitational Tournament, Women.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Ghana and South Africa scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20 Nigeria Invitational Tournament, Women.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Ghana will be considered correct if  Ghana is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to South Africa will be considered correct if South Africa is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.

If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: T20 Nigeria Invitational Tournament, Women: Ghana vs South Africa - Most Sixes Ghana Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Ghana and South Africa scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20 Nigeria Invitational Tournament, Women.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Ghana will be considered correct if Ghana is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than South Africa.The outcome corresponding to South Africa will be considered correct if South Africa is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Ghana.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: Legends Cricket League: Loser of Qualifier vs Winner of Eliminator
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Loser of Qualifier and Winner of Eliminator scheduled for March 26 2026 in Legends Cricket League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Loser of Qualifier will be considered correct if Loser of Qualifier is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Winner of Eliminator.The outcome corresponding to Winner of Eliminator will be considered correct if Winner of Eliminator is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Loser of Qualifier.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects razor-thin margins in the Legends League Cricket showdown between Bhilwara Kings (qualifier losers) and Gujarat Giants (eliminator winners) for most sixes, with all outcomes at 50%, underscoring evenly matched power-hitting arsenals. Bhilwara's Kieron Pollard and Shane Watson have cleared ropes prolifically in league play, mirroring Gujarat's Chris Gayle and David Miller, who feasted in recent knockouts. No key injuries or suspensions alter lineups, while both squads' recent momentum—Bhilwara's resilience post-loss, Gujarat's upset win—fuels volatility. T20's death-overs drama and toss-dependent pitches amplify draw potential, leaving wisdom of crowds deadlocked amid historical parity in high-scoring thrillers.

question: Legends Cricket League: Loser of Qualifier vs Winner of Eliminator - Team Top Batter Loser of Qualifier Winner
description:
This market refers to which team's player records the highest individual run total in the cricket match between Loser of Qualifier and Winner of Eliminator scheduled for 2026-03-26 in Legends Cricket League.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Loser of Qualifier will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Loser of Qualifier.
The outcome corresponding to Winner of Eliminator will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from Winner of Eliminator.
If the highest individual score is shared by at least one player from each team, the market will resolve to Draw.
The market will resolve according to which team's player records the highest individual run total regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions).
DLS/DRS adjustments and any other on-field rulings affecting batting statistics are treated as ordinary data.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official batting statistics being recorded, the market will resolve Draw.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: Will Conagra Brands (CAG) beat quarterly earnings?
description:
As of market creation, Conagra Brands is estimated to release earnings on April 1, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Conagra Brands’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.40 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Conagra Brands reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $0.40 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Conagra Brands releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

eventMetadata.context_description: Conagra Brands' (CAG) consistent EPS beats over the last four quarters, including Q1 FY2025's $0.61 actual versus $0.56 expected, underpin the 63% market-implied probability of topping Q2 consensus estimates of $0.58 per share and $2.93 billion in revenue when results land on October 10. Traders weigh this history against headwinds like persistent volume erosion in snacks amid consumer pullback and private-label rivalry, even as pricing actions and Sunrise integration bolster margins. FY25 guidance holds organic sales flat, but frozen foods resilience offers upside potential; the odds capture trader consensus on execution amid staples sector caution, with real capital reflecting moderate optimism ahead of the print.

eventMetadata.context_description: Lamb Weston Holdings (LW) traders on Polymarket price a 78% implied probability of beating Q1 FY2025 EPS consensus at $1.09, driven primarily by the company's consistent earnings outperformance—beating estimates in seven of the last eight quarters—and upwardly revised full-year guidance issued after Q4 FY2024 results on July 25. That quarter delivered adjusted EPS of $1.10 versus $0.86 expected, fueled by pricing actions and volume gains from key customers like McDonald's, despite a revenue shortfall from softer North American demand. Recent potato supply stabilization and margin expansion via cost efficiencies bolster sentiment, though risks linger from commodity volatility; traders eye the October 4 earnings release as the key resolution catalyst.

events.title: Will MSC Industrial Direct (MSM) beat quarterly earnings?
events.description:
As of market creation, MSC Industrial Direct is estimated to release earnings on April 1, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for MSC Industrial Direct’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.84 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if MSC Industrial Direct reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $0.84 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If MSC Industrial Direct releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an 88.5% implied probability for MSC Industrial Direct (MSM) beating Q1 fiscal 2025 earnings estimates, led by the distributor's eight-quarter streak of EPS beats and robust average daily sales growth reported in recent months. Supporting factors include gross margin expansion to 42.5% in the prior quarter from supply chain efficiencies and stabilizing industrial demand, as reflected in the ISM Manufacturing PMI holding above 47. Consensus projects EPS of $1.34 versus $1.18 last year, with revenue at $992 million. Upcoming October 24 release remains pivotal, where outperformance could validate premium multiples amid sector re-rating.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 68% implied probability against RH beating quarterly earnings consensus, driven primarily by persistent weakness in comparable store sales and softening luxury home furnishings demand amid elevated mortgage rates and cautious high-end consumer spending. RH's recent Q2 fiscal 2024 results showed a 9.7% comp sales drop and revenue shortfall versus estimates, with guidance signaling Q3 comp declines of 10-14% and full-year contraction of 12-16%, fueling skepticism on a rebound. Key catalysts include the upcoming Q3 earnings release around late December, where traders eye EPS thresholds against lowered analyst whispers of ~$2.80, alongside macroeconomic headwinds from sluggish housing starts and potential holiday slowdowns underscoring the market's bearish tilt.

question: Will Albania come in last place at Eurovision 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 places last in the final.

If no last place country is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules. 

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (LOW) $85 by end of March?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or below the listed price between market creation and the final trading day of March 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a modest 35% implied probability for WTI Crude Oil (CL) surpassing $85 by March 31, reflecting bearish sentiment amid surging U.S. shale output—now at record 13.2 million bpd per EIA data—and building inventories, up 4.2 million barrels last week. Countervailing forces include OPEC+ production cuts extended through Q2 and Houthi disruptions in the Red Sea, which have inflated freight costs and tightened supply chains. Key catalysts ahead: weekly EIA storage reports (Wednesdays), Fed's March 20 FOMC decision on rates impacting demand outlook, and China's Q1 GDP release March 15. Historical precedent shows CL often rallies 5-10% into quarter-end on technical buying if above $80, but recession fears cap upside.

question: Will "Project Hail Mary" score at least 93 on the Rotten Tomatoes Tomatometer?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the displayed Rotten Tomatoes “All Critics” Tomatometer score for Project Hail Mary (2026) is at least equal to the specified number at 10:00 AM ET on March 23, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If, for any reason, the resolution data is unavailable at this market's specified end time, the resolution source will be checked until the relevant data is available. This market will resolve to “No” if no data is available by March 27, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket leans toward higher Rotten Tomatoes strikes for "Project Hail Mary," with implied probabilities favoring scores above 80% driven by the film's elite pedigree: Andy Weir's bestseller (like "The Martian" at 91%), Ryan Gosling's post-"Barbie" box-office pull, and directors Phil Lord and Christopher Miller's hitmaking track record from "Spider-Verse." Production wrapped earlier this year without reported issues, fueling optimism amid sci-fi's strong critical reception for smart, effects-driven fare. No reviews exist pre its March 20, 2026, Amazon MGM release, but an imminent trailer drop could spike buzz or introduce volatility; historical precedents show unreleased hype often overpredicts Tomatometer tallies by 5-10 points.

question: Counter-Strike: NIP vs Liquid (BO3) - BLAST Open Rotterdam Group B
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Lower bracket quarterfinal 2 match between NIP and Liquid in the BLAST Open Rotterdam Group B, initially scheduled for March 21 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "NIP" if NIP win the match against Liquid.

This market will resolve to "Liquid" if Liquid win the match against NIP.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.
